thread.title: USDA denies Iowa's request to ban SNAP purchases of lab-grown proteins and egg substitutes
thread.title_full: USDA denies Iowa's request to ban SNAP purchases of lab-grown proteins and egg substitutes

author: Tyler Downey
title: USDA denies Iowa's request to ban SNAP purchases of lab-grown proteins and egg substitutes
text:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture denied Iowa's request to prohibit SNAP benefits from being used to buy lab-grown meat or egg substitutes, as [The Gazette reports](https://www.thegazette.com/state-government/feds-reject-iowa-request-to-bar-food-assistance-dollars-from-buying-meat-egg-substitutes/), recently obtained the letter denying the waiver on Wednesday.
The Iowa Department of Health and Human Services' waiver request said that cultivated protein and egg substitutes "may not" be as nutritious as meat and eggs.
Iowa Republicans in favor of the measure also raised concerns about lab-grown products putting Iowa's livestock farmers and poultry producers in jeopardy.
The USDA denied the request, saying it did not provide information on how it would raise nutrition levels for Iowans or how the state would measure the effects of lab-grown products being banned.
The USDA also said that the waiver request did not meet any of the four criteria needed for a waiver, those being:
The waiver was denied in a letter from the USDA to the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services from July 12, but The Gazette only just received the letter earlier this week.
Neither Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds nor Iowa Department of Health and Human Services Director Kelly Garcia responded to The Gazette's request for comment Thursday.
Iowa legislature passed Senate File 2391, which banned meat and egg substitutes, last spring.
The new law also bans lab-grown meat substitutes from being served in Iowa school meals.
